idea_summary: Validated serving-aligned Qwen3.5 FP8 end-to-end baseline; verification passed
Dense active-prefix scatter: iterate only M*topk routed rows and remove full MAX_SORTED_ROUTES memset; KEEP 0.301987ms -> 0.296775ms
(DISCARD) --hoist-offset --hoist-scale flags cause 40x regression - incompatible with dynamic segment addressing
(DISCARD) Preload scale_a to shared: dynamic shape DMA fell back to low-perf opt_copy API causing 40x regression
(DISCARD) Static-shape sScaleA preload: compiles OK but neutral perf (bank conflicts offset coalescing benefit)
(DISCARD) maxreg=128 to boost occupancy 18.75%→25%: register spills hurt more than occupancy helps
(DISCARD) Warp-spec 1p1c: 2 warp groups + DMA/TMA pipeline. 28% regression - producer overhead too high for small K-loop (16 iters), halved compute capacity not compensated by pipelining
(DISCARD) -bf16n compiler flag for bf16 narrow output stores: neutral perf, within noise of baseline 8.83
(DISCARD) Convert LHS load from DMA to TMA (required compiler bug fix in GenIndices for view().from() coords): neutral perf 8.77 vs baseline 8.83, TMA descriptor overhead offsets HW copy gains

(KEEP) Fuse scatter epilogue into WGMMA kernel: shared-mem staging + atomicAdd scatter replaces separate kernel launch. Serving 8.93 vs 8.84 (+1.0%), E2E 8.44 vs 8.34 (+1.2%). Also fixed earlysema.cpp null deref bug in compiler.
(DISCARD) f32 shared output_s instead of bf16: eliminates bf16 precision roundtrip but 2x shared memory caused -12% regression from worse store patterns
(KEEP) Replace serial GPU prefix sum (build_layout, 26.6us) with CPU memcpy+compute: ~8us savings. Serving 9.20 vs 8.93 (+3.0%), E2E 8.66 vs 8.44 (+2.6%)

(KEEP) Fuse quantize_input (22.5us) + sort_and_gather (26us) into single kernel: shared-mem staging eliminates intermediate global buffers and one kernel launch. Serving 9.56 vs 9.20 (+3.9%), E2E 9.00 vs 8.66 (+3.9%)
(KEEP) Fuse route + count_experts into single kernel: atomicAdd expert_counts during routing eliminates separate count kernel (4.4us). Serving 9.53 flat, E2E 9.27 vs 9.00 (+3.0%)

(KEEP) Transpose rep_a_scales layout from [SORTED_ROUTES, K_BLOCKS] to [K_BLOCKS, SORTED_ROUTES]: consecutive tokens' scales now contiguous in memory for coalesced WGMMA reads. Serving 9.65 vs 9.53 (+1.3%), E2E 9.37 vs 9.27 (+1.1%)
(DISCARD) Preload scale_a per-K-iteration into shared memory: sync.shared overhead + extra shared memory store/load cycle in K-loop negated any register pressure benefit. Serving 9.54 vs 9.65 (-1.1%)

(KEEP) Remove 23 redundant sync.shared calls in quant_sort_gather: K-loop writes to non-overlapping sq positions (15 syncs removed), scatter loop writes to non-overlapping route_slots positions (8 syncs removed). Serving 9.67 vs 9.65 (+0.2%), E2E 9.39 vs 9.37 (+0.2%)

(KEEP) Replace H→D memcpy for expert_write_offsets with D→D copy: offsets_h[i]==write_offsets_h[i] so copy from already-uploaded expert_offsets_d. Saves ~2us host-device transfer. Serving 9.75 vs 9.67 (+0.8%), E2E 9.45 vs 9.39 (+0.6%)

(KEEP) Eliminate output shared memory round-trip: scatter WGMMA accumulator directly from registers to global memory via atomicAdd, skipping mma.store→output_s→read→scatter path. Removes 9.1-way bank conflicts on shared stores and f32→bf16→f32 precision loss. Serving 9.78 vs 9.75 (+0.3%), E2E 9.48 vs 9.45 (+0.3%)

(KEEP) Enable --use_fast_math nvcc flag: faster expf/divf in routing softmax, FTZ mode avoids slow denormalized float paths, reduced instruction count. Serving 9.83 vs 9.78 (+0.5%), E2E 9.60 vs 9.48 (+1.3%)

(KEEP) Add --disable-runtime-check Choreo flag: removes choreo_assert() calls that created function call boundaries serializing WGMMA pipeline (ptxas C7510 warning eliminated). Serving 10.07 vs 9.83 (+2.4%), E2E 9.84 vs 9.60 (+2.5%)

(KEEP) Replace D2H+CPU prefix sum+H2D+D2D with GPU-side fused_moe_build_layout kernel using shared memory: eliminates host-device synchronization for expert offsets computation. Serving 10.27 vs 10.07 (+2.0%), E2E 10.01 vs 9.84 (+1.7%)

(KEEP) Fuse count_experts + build_layout into single fused_moe_count_and_build kernel using shared memory atomics: eliminates one kernel launch + cudaDeviceSynchronize + cudaMemset for expert counts. Serving 10.52 vs 10.27 (+2.4%), E2E 9.91 vs 10.01 (-1.0%, minor regression from binary size)

(KEEP) Enable --hoist-offset --hoist-scale Choreo flags: hoists loop-invariant offset/address and scale calculations out of inner loops in WGMMA kernel. Serving 10.61 vs 10.52 (+0.9%), E2E 10.02 vs 9.91 (+1.1%)

(KEEP) Remove cudaDeviceSynchronize from launch paths + use cudaMemsetAsync: eliminates host-device sync between timing iterations, allowing host to pipeline kernel launches while GPU executes. Serving 10.78 vs 10.61 (+1.6%), E2E 10.15 vs 10.02 (+1.3%)

(KEEP) Batch QSG slot assignments: move all 8 expert slot atomicAdd assignments into single inthreads block with 1 sync.shared, then do all 8 route copies without intermediate syncs. Reduces 8 sync.shared to 1. Serving 10.85 vs 10.78 (+0.6%), E2E 10.24 vs 10.15 (+0.9%)

(KEEP) Remove cudaDeviceSynchronize from 8 compiler-generated kernel wrapper functions via post-processing: since all kernels use default stream which serializes GPU execution, the host-blocking syncs between kernel launches are redundant. Eliminating them lets the CPU pipeline kernel submissions while GPU executes. Serving 11.36 vs 10.85 (+4.7%), E2E 10.93 vs 10.24 (+6.7%)

(KEEP) CUDA Graph capture for serving path: capture 3-kernel sequence (count_and_build + quant_sort_gather + grouped_wgmma_fp8) as a graph, replay with cudaGraphLaunch eliminating per-kernel CPU launch overhead and TMA tensor map recreation. Requires --default-stream per-thread flag. Serving 11.61 vs 11.36 (+2.2%), E2E 10.93 (flat, not graphed)

(KEEP) CUDA Graph capture for E2E path: capture 5-kernel E2E sequence (route + build_layout + QSG + WGMMA + memsets) as graph, replay with cudaGraphLaunch. Serving 11.61 (unchanged), E2E 11.23 vs 10.93 (+2.7%)
(KEEP) Reorder K-loop loads: issue TMA for sB before cp.async for sA so both overlap in memory pipeline. Reduces load latency per K-iter. Serving 11.67 vs 11.61 (+0.6%), E2E 11.30 vs 11.23 (+0.7%)

(KEEP) Remove unused output_s shared buffer (16KB) + integrate load overlap at .co level (TMA before DMA). Smem reduced from 42KB to 26KB. Serving 11.68 (flat), E2E 11.30 (flat) - clean-up iteration, occupancy still register-limited
(DISCARD) Force --maxrregcount=128 to increase occupancy 3→4 CTAs/SM. Register spilling outweighs occupancy gain. Serving 11.17 (-4.4%), E2E 10.81 (-4.3%)

(DISCARD) K-loop #pragma unroll 2 for compiler-assisted pipelining. ptxas sees 2 iterations (extra warpgroup.wait injected) but explicit barriers prevent cross-iter overlap. Serving 11.68 (flat), E2E 11.30 (flat)

(DISCARD) Choreo --hoist-offset --hoist-scale flags to move address offset and scale factor calculations outside K-loop. Serving 11.68 (flat), E2E 11.28 (flat). Address calculations already negligible vs memory/compute stalls.

(DISCARD) ptxas --opt-level=4 and -dlcm=cg tested separately. opt-level=4 alone flat (11.70). With -dlcm=cg slight regression (11.63). ptxas already scheduling well; bypass-L1 hurts because kernel needs L1 for scale reads.

(DISCARD) K-loop double-buffering (ping-pong sA/sB shared memory). Register pressure 168→186, dropping 3→2 CTAs/SM. Even with --maxrregcount=168 (0 spills): 10.89 (-6.8%) serving, 10.58 (-6.4%) E2E. Memory-bound kernel gains minimal overlap but pays high overhead.

(DISCARD) Convert sA from cp.async (dma.copy.swiz) to TMA to eliminate 258K smem bank conflicts. Serving 11.62 (flat/-0.5%). TMA vs cp.async is a wash for 8KB tile: TMA eliminates bank conflicts but cp.async distributes load across all threads. Also tested Choreo -vec --stmatrix and -fopt-dma flags: all flat.

(KEEP) Vectorize QSG scatter copy: replace 16-iteration byte-by-byte K-block loop with single uint4 (16-byte) store per thread. 128 threads × 16 bytes = 2048 bytes = K. Reduces 128 store instructions per thread to 8 (one per topk selection). Serving 11.74 vs 11.65 (+0.77%), E2E 11.36 vs 11.26 (+0.89%)
(DISCARD) Remove 17 redundant __syncthreads in QSG (16 in quant loop + 1 after slot assignment) by removing sync.shared after inthreads blocks. Serving 11.74 (flat), within noise of iter034.

(DISCARD) Choreo flags --ptx-barrier --branch-norm --loop-norm on iter034.co. ptxas C7510 warning: WGMMA serialization from pipeline crossing function boundary. Serving 11.65 (-0.77%), E2E 11.28 (-0.70%). Flags interfere with WGMMA pipelining.

(KEEP) Batch QSG quantization loop: save 16 BF16 input values in registers, compute all 16 K-block warp maxes without intermediate syncs, then compute all scales, then quantize all from registers. Reduces sync count from 48 to 7, enables instruction pipelining across K-blocks. Serving 11.87 vs 11.74 (+1.1%), E2E 11.47 vs 11.36 (+0.97%)

(DISCARD) Fuse count_and_build into QSG using grid-level atomic spin-wait: each block atomicAdds expert counts, last block does prefix sum, broadcasts via atomicExch, all blocks spin-wait. Serving 11.14 vs 11.87 (-6.2%), E2E 11.57. Grid-level sync spin-wait overhead far exceeds eliminated kernel launch cost.

(DISCARD) Presort topk_weights during QSG scatter phase into sorted_topk_weights[slot], use coalesced load in WGMMA scatter epilogue instead of random-access topk_weights[route_id]. Also add __ldg for sorted_route_ids read. Serving 11.89 (flat vs 11.87), E2E 11.49. Weight lookup is single float per row, negligible vs matrix data.

(KEEP) Add __restrict__ qualifier to all kernel pointer parameters via post-processing. Eliminates redundant load checks by guaranteeing no pointer aliasing. Serving 11.93 vs 11.90 (+0.21%, 500-rep avg), E2E 11.53. Consistent across all runs but marginal.

(DISCARD) Tried 6 sub-approaches: (a) K-loop commit/wait 11.92 (-0.58%), (b) force-inline copy_if_g2s 11.94 (-0.37%), (c) L2 persist sA 11.90 (-0.68%), (d) __launch_bounds__(128,3) 11.90 (-0.75%), (e) red.relaxed.gpu.global.add.f32 scatter 11.94 (flat), (f) swizzle-64 crashed. All flat/worse. Kernel at 77.8% DRAM throughput ceiling, 18.75% occupancy (168 regs), 61.5% L1TEX stalls. Micro-optimizations exhausted.

(DISCARD) Tried 4 sub-approaches: (a) __launch_bounds__(128,4) catastrophic -55% from reg spilling, (b) -Xptxas --allow-expensive-optimizations flat, (c) K-loop scale prefetch flat, (d) TMA/DMA overlap via copy_if_g2s_async flat. A/B test 3x500-rep: baseline 12.05 vs overlap 12.04 serving TFLOPS. At 80% DRAM utilization, overlapping memory ops doesn't help - bottleneck is total bandwidth not serialization.

(DISCARD) Tried 7 sub-approaches: (a) WGMMA unroll 11.92 (-1.3%), (b) WGMMA inline 11.90 (-1.1%), (c) tile-K 256 crashed (TMA box limit), (d) double-buffering abandoned (iter032 showed -6.8%), (e) --mem-reuse 11.91 (flat), (f) -sfv 11.94 (flat), (g) --use-prepack-v2 11.91 (flat). All Choreo compiler flags exhausted. Kernel at 77% DRAM throughput ceiling.

(KEEP) Rescale-accumulator pattern: eliminate mc_scale_frag dual accumulator (64 floats) by using in-place rescale (mc /= sa*sb before WGMMA, mc *= sa*sb after). Register count 168→96, occupancy 3→5 CTAs/SM (18.75%→31.25%). 5x500-rep A/B: 12.24 vs 11.92 (+2.67%), E2E 11.82 vs 11.50

(KEEP) Fused rescale: combine inverse-rescale(k) and scale(k) into single pass after WGMMA. For k<15: mc *= (sa_k*sb_k)/(sa_{k+1}*sb_{k+1}). For k=15: mc *= sa*sb. Eliminates one 64-element accumulator pass per K-iter from critical path, TMA starts earlier. Serving 12.28 vs 12.22 (+0.51%), E2E 11.85

(KEEP) TMA/DMA overlap: move TMA barrier.wait() to after DMA cp.async copy so TMA engine and LSU pipeline run in parallel. Combined with fused rescale from iter045. Serving 12.45 vs 12.31 (+1.14%), E2E 12.03

(DISCARD) Persistent kernel with work-stealing to eliminate 50% tail wave. 570 CTAs (5/SM × 114 SMs) with global atomic counter. Serving 12.42 vs 12.45 (-0.24%), E2E 12.01. Atomic counter + __syncthreads per work item overhead outweighs tail wave savings (~1.8 items/CTA).

(DISCARD) Move __ldg scale reads before WGMMA to overlap load latency with compute. Serving 12.45 (flat vs 12.45 iter046). Compiler already schedules __ldg reads effectively; no additional benefit from explicit prefetch.

(DISCARD) Replace DMA (cp.async) with TMA for A matrix, both A+B share one TMA barrier (24576 bytes). Serving 12.34 vs 12.45 (-0.88%). Regression because TMA-for-both serializes loads through same TMA engine, while DMA+TMA uses two separate hardware datapaths (LSU + TMA) in parallel.
(DISCARD) -Xptxas -dlcm=ca to force L1 caching of global loads. Serving 12.54 (flat vs 12.50 iter046). ca is already the default cache mode; no change in behavior.

(KEEP) Swap WGMMA grid from (256,4) to (4,256): groups 4 N-tile CTAs of same expert adjacently in block schedule, improving L2 temporal locality for shared sA (lhs) data. 3x500-rep A/B: baseline 12.50 vs gridswap 12.75 serving TFLOPS (+2.0%), E2E 12.29.

(KEEP) Pin lhs (rep_a_q_d, 2MB) in L2 via cudaAccessPolicyWindow with hitProp=Persisting before CUDA Graph capture. Combined with iter051 grid swap, ensures sA cp.async loads always hit L2. 3x500-rep A/B: gridswap 12.75 vs +persist 12.96 serving TFLOPS (+1.6%), E2E 12.29.

(DISCARD) Cache scale_a/scale_b reads across K-loop iterations: preload k=0 scales before loop, reuse k+1 reads as next iteration's k values. Eliminates ~45% of __ldg scale reads. 3x500-rep A/B: iter052 12.95 vs scalecache 12.97 serving TFLOPS (+0.15%, flat). Scale reads are too few (<3KB/iter) to be a bottleneck; compiler already hides latency.

(DISCARD) Double-buffer sB only (24KB→40KB SMEM) with TMA pre-issue after WGMMA to overlap TMA with rescale. No WGMMA serialization warning (single PTX instruction). But 3x500-rep A/B: 12.95 vs 12.59 serving TFLOPS (-2.9%). Regression from L1 cache pressure: 5CTAs×40KB=200KB SMEM leaves only 28KB L1 (vs 108KB at 24KB/CTA), devastating L1 hit rate for cp.async DMA reads. Also tried: (a) full double-buffer (both sA+sB, 48KB) caused ptxas WGMMA pipeline serialization from CUTE function calls; (b) TMA L2 promotion (flat, sB unique per CTA); (c) launch_bounds(128,6) requires <80 regs but WGMMA needs 90+.

(DISCARD) K-loop branch normalization: extract K-loop body into always_inline lambda with bool __is_last parameter, split loop into 0..14 (is_last=false) + explicit k=15 (is_last=true). Eliminates branch inside main loop body. 3x500-rep A/B: iter052 12.95 vs branchnorm 12.98 serving TFLOPS (+0.17%, flat). Compiler already handles the branch well; branch misprediction cost negligible vs memory latency.

(DISCARD) K-loop cleanup: hoist loop-invariant rhs_expert_n_offset and lhs_m_base, remove future wrapper objects, add pragma unroll to inner WGMMA loop, remove dead code. 3x100-rep A/B: 12.93 vs 12.93 serving TFLOPS (flat). Also tried: (a) SMEM coalesced epilogue 12.68 (-2.0%), (b) L2 persist 16MB 12.96 (+0.12%). All sub-attempts discarded.

(DISCARD) WGMMA 64x64x32: halve N-tile to 64, reduce mc[] 64->32 regs (96->64 total), sB 16KB->8KB, 8 CTAs/SM. Grid 2048 CTAs (2.25 waves). 3x200-rep: 12.41 vs 12.93 serving TFLOPS (-4.0%). Doubled CTA count and 2.25 waves (vs 1.80) increases overhead. Also tried: launch_bounds(128,4) allowing 128 regs (4 CTAs/SM), 12.65 vs 12.93 (-2.2%). 5 CTAs/SM at 96 regs is the sweet spot.

(DISCARD) Replace cuda::barrier::wait() with inline PTX mbarrier.try_wait.parity spin-wait to eliminate CS2R SR_GLOBALTIMERLO stalls (~8% of long_sb). 3x500-rep A/B: baseline 12.955 vs ptxwait 12.964 serving TFLOPS (+0.07%, flat). Timer reads were not on critical path; barrier wait time dominated by actual TMA latency, not backoff overhead.

(DISCARD) Replace atomicAdd (ATOMG.ADD with unused return value) with fire-and-forget RED.relaxed.gpu.global.add.f32 via inline PTX in epilogue scatter. SASS confirmed REDG.E.ADD replaces ATOMG. 3x500-rep A/B: baseline 12.955 vs red 12.949 serving TFLOPS (-0.05%, flat). Epilogue is not on critical path; kernel time is dominated by K-loop memory loads.

(DISCARD) Reduce TMA barrier count from 128 to 1: only leader thread arrives+waits, non-leaders skip barrier ops entirely, rely on __syncthreads(). Eliminates 127 barrier.arrive() + 127 barrier.wait() per K-iteration. 3x500-rep A/B: baseline 12.955 vs baropt 12.933 serving TFLOPS (-0.17%, flat). Barrier ops were already fast (shared memory atomics); savings negligible vs DRAM latency.

(DISCARD) Preload epilogue data (sorted_route_ids, topk_weights, output addresses) into registers before K-loop to hide memory latency behind 16 K-iterations of compute. 3x500-rep A/B: baseline 12.955 vs epilogpre 12.942 serving TFLOPS (-0.10%, flat). Epilogue load latency (~500ns) is negligible vs 156us kernel; K-loop already hides it. Still 96 regs, same occupancy.

(DISCARD) K-loop #pragma unroll 2: partially unroll K-loop body to allow compiler to interleave instructions from 2 consecutive K-iterations. Confirmed 2 warpgroup.wait injections (vs 1 baseline). 3x500-rep A/B: baseline 12.955 vs kunroll 12.974 serving TFLOPS (+0.15%, flat/noise). DRAM-bound kernel; ILP improvement negligible vs memory latency.

(DISCARD) Combine RED.ADD epilogue + K-loop #pragma unroll 2: test whether individually-flat micro-optimizations have cumulative effect. Still 96 regs, 5 CTAs/SM. 3x500-rep A/B on GPU1: baseline 12.894 vs combo 12.927 serving TFLOPS (+0.26%, flat/noise). Sub-linear combination confirms no hidden synergy.

(DISCARD) Software-pipelined TMA: issue TMA for sB[k+1] after explicit warpgroup_commit+wait but before rescale, overlapping TMA latency with rescale computation. Verified correct (test passed). 3x500-rep A/B on GPU1: baseline 12.912 vs tmapipe 12.927 serving TFLOPS (+0.12%, flat). Rescale is ~16 cycles of FMULs, negligible vs TMA latency (~1000 cycles).

(KEEP) Optimize QSG kernel: (1) pipeline 16 DRAM loads by separating load phase from reduction phase, enabling memory-level parallelism across K-blocks; (2) parallelize cross-warp max reduction from 1 thread to 16 threads (one per K-block); (3) use branchless fmaxf+__ldg instead of branchy if-else+raw loads; (4) remove redundant __syncthreads. 3x500-rep A/B on GPU1: baseline 12.886 vs qsgopt 13.139 serving TFLOPS (+1.97%).

(KEEP) Parallelize QSG slot assignment: replace thread-0-only serial loop (8 iterations of atomicAdd+store) with 8-thread parallel assignment (threads 0..7 each handle one topk selection). Reduces slot assignment from ~800 cycles to ~100 cycles per block. 3x500-rep A/B on GPU1: iter065 13.126 vs qsgpar 13.289 serving TFLOPS (+1.24%).

(KEEP) Parallel Hillis-Steele prefix scan in count_and_build: replace thread-0-only serial O(256) prefix sum with 256-thread parallel inclusive scan in O(log 256)=8 steps. Reduces scan from ~5120 to ~320 cycles. 3x500-rep A/B on GPU1: iter066 13.294 vs parscan 13.339 serving TFLOPS (+0.34%, marginal but consistent).

(KEEP) Pipeline topk_ids loads in count_and_build: batch all 4 __ldg loads before atomicAdds to exploit memory-level parallelism and hide DRAM latency. 3x500-rep A/B on GPU1: iter067 13.329 vs cabopt 13.415 serving TFLOPS (+0.65%).

(KEEP) Overlap scale factor loads with in-flight TMA: move scale_a and scale_b DRAM loads from after TMA wait to between DMA completion and TMA barrier wait, hiding scale load latency behind TMA transfer. Same 96 regs, 5 CTAs/SM. 6x500-rep A/B on GPU1: iter068 13.411 vs scaleovlp 13.466 serving TFLOPS (+0.41%, consistent across all 6 runs).

(DISCARD) Async DMA for lhs loads: replace synchronous copy_if_g2s with async variant (cp_async_fence without cp_async_wait), place scale factor loads between DMA issue and DMA wait to triple-overlap DMA+TMA+scale loads. Still 96 regs, 5 CTAs/SM. 3x500-rep A/B on GPU1: iter069 13.457 vs asyncdma 13.470 serving TFLOPS (+0.10%, flat/noise). DMA and scale loads already overlapped with TMA in iter069; making DMA explicitly async adds no additional overlap benefit.

(DISCARD) TMA double-buffering for sB in WGMMA: alternating ping-pong TMA buffers with 2 barriers to overlap TMA(k+1) with WGMMA(k). Tried 3 placement variants (tmadbl/tmadbl2/tmadbl3). All regressed -9.3% (12.21 vs 13.47 TFLOPS). Control experiment (smempad, 40KB SMEM no double-buf) showed flat, proving regression is from double-buffer logic itself (extra barrier + sB pointer alternation). NCU showed DRAM throughput dropped 85%→77% despite same data volume. Root cause: additional barrier management and pointer indirection disrupt compiler SASS scheduling for WGMMA pipeline.

(DISCARD) Hoist 8 WGMMA SMEM descriptors + loop-invariant values out of K-loop, add #pragma unroll to inner WGMMA loop, fuse epilogue weight multiplication into k=15 rescale, hoist epilogue data preload. 3x500-rep A/B on GPU1: baseline 13.449 vs hoistdesc 13.464 serving TFLOPS (+0.11%, flat/noise). Compiler already performs LICM for descriptors; weight fusion saves only 64 MULs/thread (negligible).

(DISCARD) Remove __CHOREO_DMA_DIAGNOSIS__ flag to eliminate future/TMAAtom diagnostic branch overhead in K-loop. 3x500-rep A/B on GPU1: baseline 13.471 vs nodiag 13.476 serving TFLOPS (+0.04%, flat/noise). Compiler at -O3 already eliminates unreachable diagnostic branches; removing the flag has no effect.

(DISCARD) Replace epilogue atomicAdd with PTX red.relaxed.gpu.global.add.f32 (fire-and-forget reduction, no return value). 3x500-rep A/B on GPU1: baseline 13.471 vs redrelax 13.435 serving TFLOPS (-0.27%, flat/noise). On Hopper, ATOMG and REDG have similar throughput; compiler already discards unused atomicAdd return value.

(DISCARD) __launch_bounds__(128,4) to trade occupancy (5→4 CTAs/SM) for better tail wave efficiency (77.6%→97%) and more registers (96→128). 3x500-rep A/B on GPU1: baseline 13.471 vs lbounds 13.380 serving TFLOPS (-0.68%). Reduced occupancy hurts DRAM throughput more than tail wave improvement helps on memory-bound kernel. 6 CTAs/SM impossible (WGMMA needs 90+ registers, min for 6=85).

(DISCARD) Add -Xptxas=-allow-expensive-optimizations=true flag for aggressive SASS instruction scheduling. 3x500-rep A/B on GPU1: baseline 13.471 vs expopt 13.446 serving TFLOPS (-0.19%, flat/noise). Flag had no observable effect on register usage or SASS scheduling quality.

(DISCARD) Full #pragma unroll on K-loop (16 iterations). Compiled with 96 regs (same as baseline) but runtime assertion failure: future struct state machine conflicts when 16 unrolled iterations share the same TMA barrier. The compiler reorders TMA_issue/wait across unrolled copies, breaking the sequential barrier reuse invariant.

(DISCARD) Unroll QSG output copy loop: preload all 8 slots and source data once, then #pragma unroll the 8 store iterations for better store interleaving. 3x500-rep A/B on GPU1: baseline 13.471 vs qsgcopy 13.457 serving TFLOPS (-0.10%, flat/noise). Compiler at -O3 already optimizes the copy loop; explicit unrolling adds no benefit.

(KEEP) Double QSG thread count from 128 to 256 to improve occupancy from 6.25% to 12.5%. Adapted all phases: 8 input loads/thread (vs 16), split max reduction into 2 K-block halves per iteration (8 warps, 4 per K-block), 8-byte vectorized output copies (uint2 vs uint4). 6x500-rep A/B on GPU1: iter069 13.472 vs qsg256 13.660 serving TFLOPS (+1.40%, consistent across all 6 runs).

(KEEP) Increase QSG to 512 threads for 25% occupancy. 4 iterations × 4 K-blocks per iteration, 16 warps (4 per K-block), 4-byte output copies (uint32_t). 6x500-rep A/B on GPU1: iter079 13.660 vs qsg512 13.701 serving TFLOPS (+0.30%). Cumulative +1.70% vs iter069 baseline.
